response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack/web developer with broad experience, but is a poor fit for this embedded networking security role. There is no Japanese language proficiency listed (native-level is a hard requirement), no Linux kernel modification experience, no networking/switch domain background, and no security-technology experience (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption). C experience is limited to a brief 7-month stint over a decade ago; recent work is TypeScript/Node.js product development.
strengths: Some C/C++ and low-level systems exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI, low-latency DLang services, audio/hardware thesis project)
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD) and long, consistent development track record
Comfortable with Linux, Docker, and infrastructure/dev-environment setup
concerns: No Japanese language ability listed — native-level Japanese is a mandatory requirement
No embedded networking, L2/L3 switching, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) experience
No demonstrated Linux kernel analysis/modification experience; primary recent stack is web/TypeScript, not C on UNIX
